PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO AMEND EXPERT WITNESS DESIGNATION TO SUBSTITUTE CHRISTOPHER DEPASQUALE FOR CHARLES AY (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING ONLY)
Off Calendar May-18-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Amend Expert Witness Designation To Substitute Christopher Depasquale For Charles Ay
Off Calendar May-18-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES
Daniel Morse of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs via CourtCall.
Florence McClain of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP (415-362-2580), appearing for defendant Henkel US Operation.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion to Amend Expert Witness Designation to Substitute Christopher DePasquale for Charles Ay is GRANTED under C.C.P. sections 2034.610 and 2034.620. Opposition filed by Defendant. Reply filed by Plaintiff.

Plaintiffs have shown an exceptional circumstance of the deteriorating health of designated expert, Charles Ay, from a terminal disease for the Court to permit the motion to be made under C.C.P. section 2034.610(b) after the expert discovery cutoff date. (Morse Decl. para. 6.) All four conditions are satisfied for the Court to grant leave to augment under C.C.P. section 2034.620.

Pursuant to C.C.P. section 2034.620(a), the Court considered the minimal extent to which Defendant relied on Plaintiffs' list of expert witnesses. Plaintiffs disclosed DePasquale in their initial exchange of expert witness information. (Morse Decl. para. 2; Ex. A at 2:3-21, 21:9-22:21.) The general substance of DePasquale's testimony is similar to that of Ay, which includes identification, application, removal of, and potential exposure from, any and all asbestos and asbestos-containing products from any and all types of locations, equipment, and circumstances. (Morse Decl., Ex. A at 2:17-20, 21:17-21.)

Pursuant to C.C.P. section 2034.620(b), the Court determined that Defendant will not be prejudiced in maintaining the action on the merits. Trial is set for October 16, 2023. Defendant still has the opportunity to depose DePasquale. (See Dickison v. Howen (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 1471, 1479 ["We read this statute to evidence a concern with prejudicing the party opposing the motion by adding a new expert for which that party, due to its reliance on the previous list of experts, is not prepared and cannot be prepared in time (for trial)."].)

Pursuant to C.C.P. section 2034.620(c)(1), the Court determined that Plaintiffs would not in the exercise of reasonable diligence have determined to call the expert witness in their initial exchange of expert witness information. (See Richaud v. Jennings (1993) 16 Cal.App.4th 81, 89 ["If a party's designated expert witness were to become unavailable after expert witness information had been exchanged, it is reasonable that the party would want to replace the unavailable expert with another expert."].)

Pursuant to C.C.P. section 2034.620(d), the Court conditions Plaintiffs' leave to augment on Plaintiffs making DePasquale, the new expert, available immediately for a deposition within 60 days from the date of the order, with the understanding that Plaintiffs will not amend the general substance of the testimony that Ay, the previously designated expert, was expected to give. The Court also conditions Plaintiffs' leave to augment on other terms as may be just, including but not limited to, leave for Defendant to designate additional expert witnesses or to elicit additional opinions from those previously designated, and the awarding of costs and litigation expenses to Defendant within 60 days relating to the deposition of the expert witness. Order signed in open court. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Mary Ferreira of VERITEXT LEGAL SOLUTIONS, CSR No. 10553, mferreira@calreporter.com (510-928-6268) (503/SKF)
